political.s: Boise Cascade historically leaned right, giving 96% to Republicans in 1990 and $51K in recent FEC donations with 75% Republican allocation. The company has engaged in federal lobbying and closed its dedicated government affairs office in 1991, shifting advocacy to Portland-based representatives.

charity.s: Boise Cascade focuses charitable giving on safe housing, environmental conservation, and workforce development in its Boise headquarters and across 60+ locations. The company serves as a Gold Level supporter of organizations like Habitat for Humanity and contributes volunteer hours and in-kind donations.
charity.focus: Safe housing, environmental/natural resources, workforce development

environment.s: The company claims commitment to sustainable forestry and responsible environmental practices with certifications (SFI, PEFC, FSC). However, it faced a 2002 EPA Clean Air Act settlement requiring 95% emission reductions from plywood plants and a 2026 Oregon water quality violation fine.

labor.s: Boise Cascade participates in OSHA Voluntary Protection Programs, exceeding federal safety standards. The company emphasizes an all-inclusive safety approach and cultural inclusion, though some Glassdoor reviews mention concerns about workload and overtime limitations.

dei.s: Boise Cascade has invested in DEI initiatives, appointing a Diversity & Inclusion Director and sponsoring diversity summits as late as 2023. However, the company recently declined media comment on DEI efforts amid political pressure, suggesting possible retreat from public commitment.

animals.s: Not applicable to wood products manufacturing and building materials distribution.

guns.s: Not applicable to wood products manufacturing and building materials distribution.

privacy.s: Boise Cascade maintains data privacy policies requiring GDPR and applicable law compliance with incident response and cybersecurity teams. Company policies address data protection for customers, third parties, and employees.

execPay.s: CEO Nate Jorgensen earned $7.51M in 2024, roughly average for Fortune 500 companies his size. The average employee salary is approximately $124,452, suggesting a CEO-to-employee pay ratio around 60:1.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
